title: Display profile usernames for posts/comments and require profile before posting or commenting

requirements.text: Replace principal/placeholder author strings in the feed UI with the author’s profile username for both posts and comments.

quotes: Show the person name who is posting. Instead of those random spelling

acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/FeedPostCard.tsx`, the post header shows the author’s profile `name` (username) instead of `post.author.toString().slice(0, 10)`.
In `frontend/src/components/FeedPostCard.tsx`, each rendered comment shows the comment author’s profile `name` (username) instead of `comment.author.toString().slice(0, 10)`.
If a profile lookup fails or returns null, the UI shows a clear fallback label in English (e.g., "Unknown user") rather than a truncated principal.
Avatar fallbacks use initials derived from the username when available (for both post and comment author avatars).

requirements.text: Add React Query data access to retrieve a user profile for an arbitrary principal and reuse it to resolve usernames in the feed.

quotes: Show the person name who is posting. Instead of those random spelling

acceptanceCriteria: A new hook is added in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` to fetch `getUserProfile(principal)` and cache it with a stable queryKey that includes the principal string (e.g., `['userProfile', principalStr]`).
`FeedPostCard` uses the new hook (or equivalent shared query logic) to display usernames for the post author and comment authors without introducing infinite re-render loops.
User-facing error handling remains in English and does not crash the feed if a profile fetch fails.

requirements.text: Enforce on the backend that a user must have an existing profile before they can create a post or add a comment.

quotes: Who has not prepared profile should not be allowed to post without creating profile

acceptanceCriteria: In `backend/main.mo`, `createPost` traps with a clear English message if `userProfiles.containsKey(caller)` is false.
In `backend/main.mo`, `addComment` traps with a clear English message if `userProfiles.containsKey(caller)` is false.
Existing behavior for authorized users who do have profiles remains unchanged (posts/comments are still created as before).

requirements.text: Ensure the frontend prevents posting/commenting when the current user has not created a profile, aligning the UI behavior with the backend enforcement.

quotes: Who has not prepared profile should not be allowed to post without creating profile

acceptanceCriteria: When the authenticated user has no profile, the app flow continues to block interaction via the existing profile setup modal (no route/page renders behind it).

If any posting/commenting UI can still render (edge cases), the post composer submit and comment submit actions are disabled and show an English message instructing the user to create a profile first.
If the backend returns the new "profile required" error, the frontend displays a readable English toast/error message (not raw traps).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor; implement changes in `backend/main.mo` only (no additional backend services).
Do not edit files under `frontend/src/components/ui` or the immutable hook files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Use English for all user-facing strings added or modified.
nonGoals: Implementing a full friends list or contacts endpoint for chat.
Changing authentication providers or adding third-party auth.
Redesigning the entire feed UI beyond replacing author identifiers with usernames and enforcing profile gating for post/comment creation.
